core_mechanism: characterizing the conformally-invariant continuum scaling limit of a critical discrete random system by a random fractal curve grown through the Loewner differential equation (an iteration of conformal maps) driven by a one-dimensional Brownian motion (Schramm-Loewner Evolution)
brief_reason: The abstract seeks to prove that scaling limits of critical 2D lattice systems are conformally invariant 'especially in relation to Schramm-Loewner Evolution', identifying the Brownian-driven Loewner-equation random curve as the transferable machinery underlying the exponent/dimension predictions.
